name: Snapshot the database with Airtable and Google Drive 24/7

parameters.text: =✅ Snapshot the database with Airtable and Google Drive 24/7 — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### Daily Schedule

Starts the workflow on a timer — daily at 8:00. No input data; downstream nodes fetch what they need.

Next: Load Previous Snapshot

### Load Previous Snapshot

Searches Airtable's 'Payloads' table by formula (`={LastModified} > DATEADD(NOW(), -1, 'days')`) for records the pipeline hasn't handled yet.

Ports: ①→ Fetch Changes (Google Drive) · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Airtable PAT

### Fetch Changes (Google Drive)

Searches Google Drive for matching files/folders — the discovery step before download or processing.

Ports: ①→ Normalize Records · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2

### Normalize Records

Custom JavaScript: Normalize incoming payload records to a stable schema

Next: Upsert into Google Sheets

### Upsert into Google Sheets

Upserts a row into 'Payload Tracker', matched on `itemId` — re-running with the same key updates the row instead of duplicating it.

Ports: ①→ Advance Watermark · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Advance Watermark

Custom JavaScript: Advance the incremental watermark to the newest record seen

Next: Persist Watermark (Airtable)

### Persist Watermark (Airtable)

Upserts into Airtable's 'Payloads' table, matched on `itemId` — safe to re-run without creating duplicates.

Ports: ①→ Align Field Names · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Airtable PAT

### Align Field Names

Normalizes field names so every source feeds the same downstream schema.

Next: Stamp Processing Time

### Stamp Processing Time

Stamps `processedAt` with the current time for auditing.

Next: Sort Payloads Newest First

### Sort Payloads Newest First

Orders items (newest first) so limits and digests pick the most relevant records.

Next: Save Execution Metadata

### Save Execution Metadata

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Send Summary to Telegram

### Send Summary to Telegram

Sends a Telegram message to the configured chat — the same bot can also receive replies, so this doubles as a two-way ops channel.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Telegram bot token

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Telegram

### Escalate Failure to Telegram

Sends a Telegram message to the configured chat — the same bot can also receive replies, so this doubles as a two-way ops channel.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Telegram bot token

# Snapshot the database with Airtable and Google Drive 24/7

**Pattern:** Backup & Archive · **Level:** Core · **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Snapshot the database with Airtable and Google Drive 24/7. It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual helpers is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Daily Schedule → Load Previous Snapshot → Fetch Changes (Google Drive) → Normalize Records → Upsert into Google Sheets → Advance Watermark → Persist Watermark (Airtable) → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to backup & archive without manual effort
- Anyone automating helpers in software development
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Saves hours of manual work each week
- Responds in seconds
- Improves response time

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Airtable PAT; Google OAuth2; Google OAuth2 / service account; Telegram bot token.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

The pipeline is incremental, not full-refresh: it stores a watermark (the last-seen timestamp) and only asks the source for records newer than that, which is what keeps a scheduled re-run cheap instead of re-pulling the entire dataset every time. The tradeoff is that a failed watermark write must be safe to retry — which is exactly why the destination write is an upsert, not a plain insert.

## Practice exercises

Once it's wired up with real credentials, try:

1. Manually roll the watermark back and re-run — confirm the upsert makes the replay safe (no duplicates).
2. Trigger a reconcile mismatch on purpose and confirm the drift alarm fires.
3. Point the source at a different platform and see how little else in the workflow has to change.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your helpers process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
